deliverable: TestCrashInjectionRecovery (issue #3's literal required test name) plus 4 supporting new tests, closing all 4 orchestrator-flagged deferred gaps (a)-(d) from 1.3.1/1.3.2/1.3.4's verifications.

gaps_closed.a: OpenWriter's resume path now reads+validates the resumed segment via a new shared parseSegmentRecords helper, truncating a torn tail or failing closed on genuine CRC corruption, before reopening for append. Test: engine/wal/writer_test.go:TestOpenWriterResumeTornTailDiscardsAndTruncates.
gaps_closed.b: engine/wal/crash_subprocess_test.go:TestFsyncDurabilitySubprocessCrash — subprocess re-exec + self-SIGKILL immediately after Append returns; parent process re-reads from a separate process afterward. Documented limitation: proves absence of in-Go/process-local buffering, not physical-disk-level fsync durability (would need power-loss/VM fault injection).
gaps_closed.c: engine/wal/recovery_test.go:TestReplayCRCCorruption — flips one payload byte mid-log; Replay returns a hard CRC error at exactly that record, having applied all prior records. Also fixed a real bug found along the way: Replay previously discarded already-parsed valid records when readSegmentFrom returned any error, instead of applying them first.
gaps_closed.d: Added Writer.Offset() int64 (minimal, mutex-guarded getter mirroring SegmentNum()).

issue_acceptance_criteria_test: engine/wal/recovery_test.go:TestCrashInjectionRecovery (torn payload at tail + torn header at tail subtests) — matches issue #3's literal required test name and acceptance criteria (torn record detected and discarded, recovery proceeds from last valid record, no panic).
defensive_addition_beyond_scope: engine/wal/recovery_test.go:TestCrashInjectionRecoveryTornTailInNonLastSegmentErrors — Replay now hard-errors if a torn tail appears in a non-last segment (only the actively-written last segment can legitimately have one after a real crash).
self_consistency: .cdr/runs/2026-07-04/043-implementation/self-consistency.json — go build/vet/gofmt clean; go test ./engine/wal/... -race -v -count=1 all green (22 top-level tests, 0 regressions); -count=3 full-suite and 5x standalone repeats of the subprocess-kill test all green (no flakiness observed).
not_done_here: docs/LLD/wal.md LLD-sync (flagged as a compounding low-risk gap across 1.3.1-1.3.4's verifications; still open, explicitly out of this subtask's scope which is the crash-injection test itself)
No wiring of engine/wal into engine/catalog or engine/btree (still not in scope per docs/LLD/wal.md's 'not yet wired' status)
next_step: Hand off to /cdr:verify for subtask 1.3.5. If verification passes, issue #3 (all 5 subtasks: 1.3.1-1.3.5) should be closable.
